summary: I read the requirements (R1–R13), discovery notes, open-question defaults, and both design guides, then inspected the actual layouts, sidebar partial, Tailwind CSS entry, `status_helper.rb`, `User` model/schema, importmap, and CSP config to ground the design in real files. I wrote `.pipeliner/phases/02-plan/main/design-writer/output/design.md`, which: settles the open decisions (per-device cookie persistence, two-state toggle where cookie-presence itself encodes "manual override," zero-flash via a synchronous inline `<head>` script, WCAG AA target); proposes a Tailwind v4 class-based dark strategy (`@custom-variant dark`) built on CSS-variable semantic tokens (`bg-surface`, `text-default`, etc.) so the ~23 views get token swaps instead of per-view `dark:` duplication, per the UI guide's own directive; adds a `ThemeHelper`, an inline init-script partial, the app's first Stimulus controller, and a toggle partial rendered in both the sidebar and the auth layout; extends the already-centralized `status_helper.rb` for dark status badges; and includes a full file-level plan, a testing plan (including the app's first system test), a required `guides/ui-style-guide.md` update, and flagged follow-up risks (CSP nonce, final dark shade values, future cross-device seam) — each section cites the specific requirements it satisfies.
